8(a) BOOTCAMP OUTLINE — making the most of the 9-year 8(a) program (curriculum overview)

Purpose of 8(a): assist small disadvantaged companies to excel and expand in the federal marketplace. It requires a lot of paperwork/computer work to be successful. ~6,000 total 8(a) companies, ~300 newly minted per year. The 9-year program has two periods: Years 1–4 (Development) and Years 5–9 (Growth/Transition).

What is an 8(a)? A small business (revenue roughly $100K–$1B depending on size standard) owned by minorities, women, or Native Alaskan/Tribal entities. Graduation occurs at the size standard.

IMPORTANT SYSTEMS TO KNOW:
- SAM (System for Award Management)
- SBA Set-Aside Certifications
- GSA Multiple Award Schedule (MAS)
- Billing systems: WAWF (Wide Area Workflow) and other government systems
- CPARS (Contractor Performance Assessment Reports)

PROCUREMENT VEHICLES & PROPOSAL SERVICES:
- IDIQ (Indefinite Delivery / Indefinite Quantity) contracts
- GWACs (Government-Wide Acquisition Contracts)
- MATOCs (Multiple Award Task Order Contracts)
- BPAs (Blanket Purchase Agreements)
- IDV and Sole Source vs 8(a) Set-Aside
- Unrestricted and Small Business Set-Aside proposal responses

TYPES OF CONTRACTS / RELATIONSHIPS: Subcontract; Joint Venture; Mentor-Protégé; Prime Contract.

NAICS CODES — North American Industry Classification System codes define government business opportunities; understand why they matter, how to get them approved, and how to expand potential opportunities by adding codes.

8(a) STRATEGY & GROWTH: business parameters in Years 1–5 vs Years 6–9; bidding and information searches with auto-updates; how to complete a bid (subcontracting, JV, Mentor-Protégé, prime) and the time/resources needed to stand out; legal/compliance to keep your designation active; how to graduate from 8(a) early; a good time mix between marketing and bidding RFP/RFQ.

DIRECT MARKETING TO POCs: how to pursue direct marketing to purchasing officers/POCs; how to engage for a sole-source contract; strategies to engage the "right" POCs; build a valuable network and use it to get ahead. Walk before you run — 10 steps to 8(a) viability; performance is the key to future sole-source awards.

8(a) vs HUBZone vs WOSB vs VOSB vs Small Business: differences and advantages of each designation. 8(a) compliance covers education, revenue, and concentration of business.

Practitioner additions: respond to Sources Sought notices and devise a strategy with your SBA Business Opportunity Specialist (BOS) to request the agency consider a sole-source award; if the BOS isn't responsive, escalate to the PCR (Procurement Center Representative). Note: SBA may scrutinize working with a consultant (e.g. concerns about inflating a contract bid to cover consultant fees). Emphasize direct-marketing-to-POCs, Mentor-Protégé caution, and how to complete a competitive bid.
